# Transcrição: 01 How to Set Up Product Catalogues

- **Fonte:** 01 How to Set Up Product Catalogues

## [00:00 - 03:05]

Here's how to create a product catalog inside your business manager. So this all takes place inside your business manager settings, so navigate there now. Once you're there, you need to make sure that your pixel is owned by your business manager or you have had it assigned to your business manager by your client. And that it is, it's not just through the ad account itself, the pixel has been shared. If that is ready to go, then all you need to do on the left hand side on the menu is navigate to catalogs. This may be a different menu for you, but it will still be called catalogs or product catalogs. What you then need to do is hit the add button at the top and create a brand new catalog. For this, just demonstration, I'm just going to name it product catalogs, but obviously stick in mind with your regular naming suggestions. Select your type. I'm going to leave it as products. There are six in total, but these will just be related to whatever vertical that you're working in. And I'm going to create my catalog. Now I can associate my employees to this catalog. I don't have any in this business manager, but I can choose them to be admins who have full setting control over the catalog or advertisers who can advertise with this catalog. If they don't have advertiser access, they can't use it. So make sure that you've chosen them. Now I associate my data sources from pixels and apps. I select which ones I want. If your pixel doesn't appear here, you need to double check that you do have the right level of access to it. Let's save and continue. And now we're ready to add the data sources that we need to add. Up the top here, we're just going to add our product catalog. We're going to open our product catalog and make the source. Head over to data sources. Add a data source. Here is where you can get some additional information to help you make your data source with CSV templates or step-by-step guides, as well as plugins for a couple of major e-commerce platforms. If you're going to be uploading manually with a CSV template, just upload once. If it's from a file, set your schedule. Paste in your feed. Choose your scheduling. Enter your data feed name. If you're having multiple data feeds, do keep a naming convention so they're easy to tell. And select your default currency. Make sure that this matches the pixel reporting currency. So mine's a New Zealand dollars. I will select New Zealand dollars. I'm now going to start my upload. Now I do have a different catalog here with those products already selected just to skip the uploading time. Let's navigate over there now. And here's my data source ready to go. You will have error messages if there's any problems with your feed. So make sure that you get those resolved. There are two categories of error. The yellow and red. Red means the products have failed to upload entirely. Yellow means that Facebook would like some additional data in order to have maximum performance, but they are still usable. If you can, do resolve those. Well if you have followed these step by step instructions correctly, you now have products in this data feed. They're appearing under the product page, which they are here.

## [03:05 - 03:17]

And then under settings, you have your event source correctly associated and ready to go. Your product catalog is now usable. And you can go off and create the Facebook ads that you need.
